overview.description: AI-powered routing and navigation engine for cycling, micromobility, and Mobility-as-a-Service
overview.description_long: Umotional builds next-generation AI solutions for transport and mobility. Their two main products are Cyclers Navigate — a routing and navigation API/SDK built exclusively for cycling and micromobility, validated by 200,000+ users — and AnyRoute, an intermodal routing engine for public transport and MaaS platforms. AnyRoute powers PID Lítačka, the largest integrated transport system in Czechia serving 3 million people. Umotional's routing engine outperforms Google Maps (74% vs 12% route accuracy on real cyclist trajectories).

history.origin_story: Founded in Prague by Michal Jakob to apply AI to transport and mobility optimization problems

key_milestones.event: Launched Cyclers Navigate API/SDK for cycling and micromobility
AnyRoute deployed to power PID Lítačka — Prague Integrated Transport serving 3 million people

notable_people.name: Jan Hrnčíř
notable_people.role: Senior Java Backend and DevOps Engineer

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: Umotional's routing engine is built on proprietary community data and specialized ML algorithms for cycling/micromobility, outperforming Google Maps on cyclist trajectory accuracy. However, as foundation models and large mapping platforms (Google, Apple, Mapbox) continue investing in specialized routing, the moat could narrow over time.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: Transport and mobility is a large, real market with proven demand. AnyRoute's deployment for PID Lítačka (3 million users) and Cyclers' 200,000+ validated users demonstrate clear product-market fit. Micromobility and MaaS are growing globally.
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: No public funding information found. Company is small (11-50 employees), privately held with no disclosed investors or rounds. Revenue sustainability is unknown for a company of this size serving primarily B2B API customers.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: Umotional has genuine technical differentiation and proven enterprise clients, but the small team size and lack of disclosed funding create meaningful execution and sustainability risk. The growing MaaS market provides a favorable tailwind.

metadata.notes: No CNAIP URL available. Company has two main products: Cyclers (cycling/micromobility routing) and AnyRoute (intermodal MaaS routing). No careers page found on website. LinkedIn shows ~10 visible employees within 11-50 range.
